name: Logo Strip
description: Scrolling or static partner logo strip used for trust-building sections on marketing pages.

marquee.description: Single horizontal strip of muted partner logos scrolling slowly across the viewport.

slots.eyebrow: Optional trust label such as Trusted by leading teams
slots.logo-row: Continuous row of logo marks or wordmarks

static.description: Wrapped static grid of partner logos for lighter-weight landing pages.

slots.logo-item: Centered logo mark or wordmark tile

visual_brief: Low-noise trust strip with monochrome or softly muted brand marks sitting on a clean background. Logos should read as social proof, not as primary content. Spacing is generous, alignment is precise, and the overall effect is polished and calm rather than flashy.

responsive.mobile: Reduce visible logo count and prefer static wrapping if marquee would feel cramped. Keep marks legible and centered.
responsive.tablet: Marquee or static grid both work. Maintain even spacing and avoid oversized logo marks.
responsive.desktop: Wide horizontal strip with balanced breathing room between logos and an optional understated eyebrow label.

layout_hints.trust_hierarchy: The strip is supportive proof between primary narrative sections. It should never overwhelm the hero or compete with main CTAs.
layout_hints.logo_treatment: Use consistent box alignment and normalized visual weight across logos. Avoid loud color unless the theme explicitly calls for it.
layout_hints.section_rhythm: Parent marketing layout owns the space around the strip. This pattern owns the internal row or wrapped grid only.

motion.micro: Logo opacity may lift slightly on hover or focus when the marks are interactive.
motion.transitions: Marquee motion should be slow, linear, and unobtrusive. Static variants may fade in gently with the rest of the page.

keyboard: If logos link outward, tab through them in reading order with clear focus styles.
announcements: Expose logo names as accessible labels rather than relying on decorative images alone.
